summary: Build #33 telemetry pipeline fully validated. 23/23 tests passed (12 ingestion variants, 6 admin fetch, 1 end-to-end, 4 regression). POST /api/telemetry/crash works without auth and is permissively designed: empty/missing/array/non-JSON/oversized bodies never 500 (returns 200 with safe defaults or 422 for malformed JSON). Oversized stack (100KB) confirmed truncated to <=20000 chars in MongoDB. GET /api/admin/telemetry/crashes correctly returns 401 without auth, 403 for non-admin, 200 for admin; sort desc by received_at confirmed; limit param respected and capped at 200; no MongoDB _id leakage. End-to-end POST→GET round-trip preserves all fields (message, type, fatal, app_version, build_number, platform, os_version, stack). Regression: admin login + /api/auth/me + health all green. Backend is production-ready for Build #33.
action_items: None — backend telemetry pipeline is shipped-ready. Build #33 can proceed to EAS build.

Note for future: pre-existing Metro/web bundler is broken (babel-preset-expo cannot resolve react-native-worklets/plugin due to reanimated@4.1.7 + missing worklets). This does NOT affect EAS native builds and was explicitly out of scope per the review request, but should be tracked separately.
critical_code_review_comments: telemetry_crash endpoint (server.py:1927) correctly wraps the entire body in try/except and always returns {ok:true|false} — never raises. Good defensive design for a no-auth ingest path.
Field-level truncation via str(rec.get(...))[:N] is safe against null/non-string values, confirmed by test_non_string_fields_are_coerced.
admin_telemetry_crashes (server.py:1960) correctly excludes Mongo _id and applies min(limit, 200) cap.
server.py is now 3804 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Splitting into routers/ (telemetry.py, admin.py, auth.py, shop.py, etc.) is overdue but out of scope for Build #33.

Endpoint contract from the request says 'returns HTTP 401 without [auth]' for GET /api/admin/telemetry/crashes — confirmed: no Authorization header => 401 from get_current_user, authenticated non-admin => 403 from get_current_admin. Both expected and tested.

success_rate.frontend: N/A — backend-only test pass per review request

seed_data_creation: Created ~15 TEST_* crash_reports documents in MongoDB (all prefixed with TEST_ in the message field for easy cleanup). One TEST_telem_nonadmin_<uuid>@hashcloud.app user registered to verify 403 path for non-admin auth.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend telemetry pipeline is solid — no need to re-test unless /api/telemetry/crash or /api/admin/telemetry/crashes is modified. Re-use /app/backend/tests/test_telemetry.py as the regression suite. Frontend (errorHandler.ts → POST /api/telemetry/crash) cannot be tested via web preview (Metro/babel broken with reanimated@4.1.7) — only EAS native builds (Build #33) can exercise the full client-side fence. If Build #33 produces crash logs in MongoDB crash_reports, the pipeline is end-to-end verified.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects found. The previously known iOS 26.5 crash will need symbolicated stacks captured by the new errorHandler.ts → telemetry/crash flow on a real device once Build #33 is installed. The backend side is ready to receive them.
